messages.content:
You are a question answering assistant. Your job is to generate a SPARQL query to answer a given user question.

You should follow a step-by-step approach to generate the SPARQL query:
1. Determine possible entities and properties implied by the user question.
2. Search for the entities and properties in the knowledge graphs. Where applicable, constrain the searches with already identified entities and properties.
3. Gradually build up the SPARQL query using the identified entities and properties. Start with simple queries and add more complexity as needed. Execute intermediate queries to get feedback and to verify your assumptions. You may need to refine or rethink your current plan based on the query results and go back to step 2 if needed, possibly multiple times.
4. Use the answer or cancel function to finalize your answer and stop the generation process.

Available knowledge graphs:
- "wikidata" at https://qlever.dev/api/wikidata
  - "entity" index (type="fuzzy"): Entities indexed by their labels and synonyms
  - "property" index (type="embedding", modalities="text"): Properties indexed by their labels, synonyms, and identifiers

Index types used:
- "embedding": Retrieves items by cosine similarity between their embeddings and the query embedding. The embedding model used depends on the index and may support text, images, or both.
- "fuzzy": Retrieves items by overlap between their label words and the query keywords. The query keywords must not match label words exactly, but some fuzziness is allowed. The longer a query keyword is, the more it can deviate from a label word and still be considered a match, though it will also contribute less to the overall score. No special query operators like AND/OR are supported.

SPARQL prefixes for use in function calls:
- bd: http://www.bigdata.com/rdf#
- cc: http://creativecommons.org/ns#
- data: https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Special:EntityData/
- dc: http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/
- dct: http://purl.org/dc/terms/
- foaf: http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/
- geo: http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#
- geof: http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/
- gn: http://www.geonames.org/ontology#
- hint: http://www.bigdata.com/queryHints#
- imdb: https://www.imdb.com/
- math: http://www.w3.org/2005/xpath-functions/math#
- ontolex: http://www.w3.org/ns/lemon/ontolex#
- owl: http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#
- p: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/
- pq: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/
- pqn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value-normalized/
- pqv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value/
- pr: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/
- prn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value-normalized/
- prov: http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#
- prv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value/
- ps: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/
- psn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value-normalized/
- psv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value/
- qb: http://purl.org/linked-data/cube#
- qfn: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/function#
- ql: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/builtin-functions/
- qlss: https://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/spatialSearch/
- rdf: http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#
- rdfs: http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#
- ref: http://www.wikidata.org/reference/
- s: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/statement/
- schema: http://schema.org/
- skos: http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#
- v: http://www.wikidata.org/value/
- void: http://rdfs.org/ns/void#
- wd: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/
- wdno: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/novalue/
- wdt: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct/
- wdtn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct-normalized/
- wikibase: http://wikiba.se/ontology#
- xsd: http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#

Additional rules to follow:
- Explain your thought process before and after each step and function call.
- Do not ask the user for clarification until you have made an initial attempt at completing the task. When the task input is incomplete or ambiguous, proceed based on reasonable assumptions.
- Do not use "SERVICE wikibase:label { bd:serviceParam wikibase:language ..." in SPARQL queries. It is unsupported by the used QLever SPARQL endpoints. Use rdfs:label or similar properties to get labels instead.
- Always execute your final SPARQL query before giving an answer to make sure it returns the expected results.
- The SPARQL query should always return the actual identifiers / IRIs of the items in its result. It additionally may return labels or other human-readable information, but they are optional and should be put within optional clauses unless explicitly requested by the user.
- Do not stop early if there are still obvious improvements to be made to the SPARQL query. For example, keep refining your SPARQL query if its result contains irrelevant items or is missing items you expected.
- Do not perform additional computation (e.g. filtering, sorting, calculations) on the result of the SPARQL query to determine the answer. All computation should be done solely within SPARQL.
- For questions with a "True" or "False" answer the SPARQL query should be an ASK query.

Sort the people who worked for the Brentford football club and were born in or before 1956 by their birth year from oldest to youngest. What is the sum of the number of seasons the person who ranks second managed the club and the total appearances for the club by the player born in 1979

content.message:
I need to find information about people who worked for the Brentford football club, were born in or before 1956, and then calculate a specific sum based on the second-ranked person and the player born in 1979.

First, I need to identify the entities and properties involved:
- Brentford football club (likely an entity in Wikidata)
- People who worked for the club (could be managers or players)
- Birth year information (property likely related to birth)
- Seasons managed (property related to management)
- Total appearances (property related to player statistics)

Let me start by searching for the Brentford football club entity.

tool_calls.name: search_entity
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 entity alternatives:
1. Brentford F.C. (wd:Q19571, matched via "Brentford Football Club"), also known as "BFC", "Brentford", "Brentford FC", "Brentford Football Club", "The Bees":
  - association football club in London, England
  - is instance of association football club
2. Brentwood Town F.C. (wd:Q4961799, matched via "Brentwood Town Football Club"), also known as "Brentwood Town Football Club":
  - association football club in England
  - is instance of association football club
3. Retford United F.C. (wd:Q8992572, matched via "Retford United Football Club"), also known as "Retford", "Retford United", "Retford United FC", "Retford United Football Club", "RUFC", etc.:
  - association football club in Retford, England
  - is instance of association football club
4. Broadford Football Club (wd:Q4972178):
  - Australian Rules Football club in Victoria, Australia
5. Bedford Town F.C. (wd:Q1909532, matched via "Bedford Town Football Club"), also known as "Bedford Town Football Club":
  - association football club in England
  - is instance of association football club
6. Real Bedford F.C. (wd:Q4879143, matched via "Real Bedford Football Club"), also known as "Bedford F.C.", "Real Bedford Football Club":
  - association football club in England
  - is instance of association football club
7. Hertford Rugby Football Club (wd:Q5744712):
  - is instance of rugby union team
  - rugby team
8. Broadford Kangaroos (wd:Q112815593, matched via "Broadford Kangaroos Football Club"), also known as "Broadford Kangaroos Football Club":
  - Australian rules football club
  - is instance of Australian rules football club
9. Bradford City A.F.C. (wd:Q48879, matched via "Bradford City Association Football Club"), also known as "BCAFC", "Bradford", "Bradford City", "Bradford City AFC", "Bradford City Association Football Club", etc.:

  - is instance of association football club
10. Club Sando F.C. (wd:Q24896901, matched via "Club Sando Football Club"), also known as "Club Sando Football Club":

  - is instance of association football club

content.message:
I found the Brentford football club entity. The top result is "Brentford F.C. (wd:Q19571)" which is the association football club in London, England. I'll use this entity.

Now, I need to find people who worked for this club and were born in or before 1956. To do this, I need to search for properties that relate to people working for the club (managers or players) and their birth dates.

Let me search for properties related to birth date or birth year.

tool_calls.name: search_property
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. place of birth (wd:P19 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "born"), also known as "birth city", "birth location", "birth place", "birthplace", "born", etc.:
  - most specific known birth location of a person, animal or fictional character
2. date of birth (wd:P569 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "born"), also known as "birth date", "birthdate", "birth year", "birthyear", "born", etc.:
  - date on which the subject was born
3. birth name (wd:P1477 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "born as"), also known as "birthname", "bn", "bname", "born as", "full birth name", etc.:
  - full name of a person at birth, if different from their current, generally used name
4. birthday (wd:P3150 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "birth day"), also known as "b-day", "bday", "birth day":
  - item for day and month on which the subject was born. Used when full "date of birth" (P569) isn't known.
5. child (wd:P40 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "offspring"), also known as "children", "daughter", "daughters", "descendants", "father of", etc.:
  - subject has object as child. Do not use for stepchildren—use "relative" (P1038), qualified with "kinship to subject" (P1039)
6. commemorates (wd:P547 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "birthday of"), also known as "anniversary of", "birthday of", "celebrates", "commemorandum", "honors", etc.:
  - what or whom is commemorated by the place, monument, memorial, or holiday
7. birth rate (wd:P8763 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv):
  - the total number of live births per 1,000 population
8. date of baptism (wd:P1636 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "date of christening"), also known as "baptised", "baptism date", "baptized", "christened", "christening date", etc.:
  - date when a person was baptized. For times when only baptism ... available, this can be a good substitute for date of birth.
9. spouse (wd:P26 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "marry"), also known as "common-law husband", "common-law spouse", "common-law wife", "husband", "husbands", etc.:
  - the subject has the object as their spouse (husband, wife, ... Use "unmarried partner" (P451) for non-married companions
10. gestation period (wd:P3063 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv):
  - amount of time needed for gestation for this animal taxon

content.message:
I found the relevant property for birth date. The "date of birth" property (wd:P569) is what I need to use to filter people born in or before 1956.

content.message: I don't see a direct property for "manage football club". Let me try searching for properties related to managing or being employed at a football club.

tool_calls.name: search_property
tool_calls.result:

7. coach of sports team (wd:P6087 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "manager of"), also known as "club manager of", "club manager of sports team", "coaches", "coach of", "coach of sports club", etc.:

I found relevant properties for managing sports teams. The "head coach" property (wd:P286) and "coach of sports team" property (wd:P6087) are relevant. I think "coach of sports team" (wd:P6087) is the most specific property for someone who managed a sports team.

Now, I need to search for properties related to player appearances or statistics.

content.message:
I found the relevant property! The "head coach" property (wd:P286) is associated with the Brentford football club. This confirms that people who managed the club can be found using wd:P286.

Now I have all the necessary components to build the query. I need to:

1. Find all people who were head coaches of Brentford F.C. (wd:Q19571) and were born in or before 1956
2. Sort them by birth year (oldest to youngest)
3. For the person who ranks second, get the total number of seasons they managed the club
4. For the player born in 1979, get their total appearances
5. Sum these two values
